entry.title: Re: How the ABAP Test Cockpit supports you to adopt ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Olga,

Thanks for the blog!

Could you please let me know which ATC Variant and how can we execute that for development done directly in BTP ABAP Environment to make sure we are compatible for Clean Core. We have few RAP developments where we have created Behavior implementations in which we are calling some unreleased BAPI's from on-prem system through Cloud destinations but the ATC variant - ABAP_CLOUD_DEVELOPMENT_DEFAULT but its not giving error saying Usage of unreleased BAPI.

Thanks,

Anurag

entry.title: Re: Failed to call the endpoint: HTTP 0 null
entry.content: Which thread?

entry.title: Re: how to change the equipment structure
entry.content:
Hi,

Go to IE02, in the structure tab click on change install location.

Regards

entry.title: Re: No Create Subscription button in serverless function
entry.content: this probably works. but I would add a 6th step: Go Back to Modules Overview and Check 'State' of "eventing" module. Since at least for me this takes quite a while to activate. Until then its not actually active

we would like to import the interaction type "Pre OptIn for Marketing Permission (MKT_PERM_OPTIN_PRE)" to trigger a DOI email .

But we get the following error message: 



Can someone help us with this? 

Thanks in advance!
Katharina

entry.title: BO 4.3 Cloud: Why doesn't the organize option appear in the favorites folder on launchpad cloud?

BO 4.3 Cloud: In Launchpad, the Organize option does not appear in the favorites folder, however, this option exists in OnPremisse.

Does anyone know why we don't have the option to organize in the favorites folder in the cloud?

OnPremisse

Cloud

Thanks.

entry.title: c# crystal reports 2015 one report - 10 pages
entry.content:
Hello.

I am using VS2015 with Crystal reports for  VS2015 - CRforVS_13_0_18.exe.

I want creat labels, which currently works fine, but have strange "error",

For every report, create me 10 pages, can resolve problem.

I could say, Yes, print me only first page, but this is for me not resolve, it is bypass.

I was trying with section expert, etc..., but without proper solution.

Does some have any other idea?

Best regards,

Marko

entry.title: Re: ABAP2XLSX change comment size
entry.content:
this is the XML generated in this part:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<xml xmlns:v="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:vml" xmlns:o="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:office" xmlns:x="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:excel">
<o:shapelayout v:ext="edit">
<o:idmap v:ext="edit" data="1"/>
</o:shapelayout>
<v:shapetype id="_x0000_t202" coordsize="21600,21600" o:spt="202" path="m,l,21600r21600,l21600,xe">
<v:stroke joinstyle="miter"/>
<v:path gradientshapeok="t" o:connecttype="rect"/>
</v:shapetype>
<v:shape id="_x0000_s1025 " type="#_x0000_t202" style="size:auto;width:auto;height:auto;position:absolute;margin-left:117pt;margin-top:172.5pt;z-index:1;visibility:hidden" fillcolor="#ffffe1" o:insetmode="auto">
<v:fill color2="#ffffe1"/>
<v:shadow on="t" color="black" obscured="t"/>
<v:path o:connecttype="none"/>
<v:textbox style="mso-direction-alt:auto">
<div style="text-align:left"/>
</v:textbox>
<x:ClientData ObjectType="Note">
<x:MoveWithCells/>
<x:SizeWithCells/>
<x:Anchor>2, 15, 11, 10, 4, 31, 15, 9</x:Anchor>
<x:AutoFill>False</x:AutoFill>
<x:Row>9 </x:Row>
<x:Column>2 </x:Column>
</x:ClientData>
</v:shape>
<v:shape id="_x0000_s1026 " type="#_x0000_t202" style="size:auto;width:auto;height:auto;position:absolute;margin-left:117pt;margin-top:172.5pt;z-index:1;visibility:hidden" fillcolor="#ffffe1" o:insetmode="auto">
<v:fill color2="#ffffe1"/>
<v:shadow on="t" color="black" obscured="t"/>
<v:path o:connecttype="none"/>
<v:textbox style="mso-direction-alt:auto">
<div style="text-align:left"/>
</v:textbox>
<x:ClientData ObjectType="Note">
<x:MoveWithCells/>
<x:SizeWithCells/>
<x:Anchor>2, 15, 11, 10, 4, 31, 15, 9</x:Anchor>
<x:AutoFill>False</x:AutoFill>
<x:Row>9 </x:Row>
<x:Column>3 </x:Column>
</x:ClientData>
</v:shape>
</xml>

entry.title: Re: SAP BUSINESS ONE DI API crashes Application
entry.content:
My thought was, that (if I am not horribly mistaken) Excel also uses com. At least in my .NET application my Excel driver is com.

Also, using releaseComObject is not necessary but is recommended with .NET at least.

entry.title: Re: 4E9 Scope item-Error NR/751 in Manage Project Billing...
entry.content:
Possible reasons are

  • Billing date is in future for which FI period is not open
  • Wrong billing date is filled due to custom code 
  • Wrong billing date is passed via interface

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 4 - Mobile device capabilities
entry.content:
but i used my personal email account. not sure what is the problem.
@eshrinivasan  Do we need to complete this challenge by 30th april only? or we can extend?

entry.title: Re: Problem with Select-Options
entry.content:
Here's the code for F4 Help 

DATA: lt_return TYPE TABLE OF ddshretval,
        ls_range  LIKE s_tabnam,
        lt_dynp   TYPE TABLE OF dynpread.

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4_FIELD_ON_VALUE_REQUEST'
    EXPORTING
      tabname           = 'Table Name'
      fieldname         = 'TABNAME'
      searchhelp        = 'Search Help Name'
      dynprofield       = 'S_TABNAM'
      multiple_choice   = 'X'
    TABLES
      return_tab        = lt_return
    EXCEPTIONS
      field_not_found   = 1
      no_help_for_field = 2
      inconsistent_help = 3
      no_values_found   = 4
      OTHERS            = 5.

  IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lt_return IS NOT INITIAL. 
    LOOP AT lt_return INTO DATA(ls_return).
      s_tabnam-low = ls_return-fieldval.
      s_tabnam-sign = 'I'.
      s_tabnam-option = 'EQ'.
      APPEND s_tabnam.
      CLEAR: ls_return, s_tabnam.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative May 2024 Bootcamp Guide
entry.content: My email settings is set to allow communication. Don't send me any community emails box is not checked. Is there another way to communicate since it isn't giving you an option to reach out to me.

entry.title: Re: Error - VLPOD - Goods issue for delivery is not possi...
entry.content: You need to remove delivery block from outbound delivery that was created.  Probably, it causes relevant error. Initially, you could check from LIKP table for relevant delivery(Technical name: LIKP-LIFSK).

entry.title: Grupo no Skype para Troca de Conhecimento
entry.content:
Bom Dia, 

Galera do Brasil, criei um grupo no skype para troca de informações, dúvidas, processos, erros, enfim, como se fosse um suporte aberto.

Acredito que com a vivência e conhecimento de cada um, podemos nos ajudar antes de acionar a consultoria.

Vou deixar o link abaixo do grupo.
https://join.skype.com/oDgJbgAkDyri

entry.title: Re: How to show custom Data in a Fiori App (RAP)

entry.title: Re: Problem with Select-Options
entry.content:
I suppose this code is part of a AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST on the SELECT-OPTIONS

  • Could you try to wrap the call of F4_FIELD_ON_VALUE_REQUEST between a call of FM RS_SELECTIONSCREEN_READ (read before) and of RS_SELECTIONSCREEN_UPDATE (update after) - replacing the code that add records to the select-options without updating the dynpro

entry.title: PO Archiving
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

1. PO archiving residence time 1 & residence time 2 - Can maintain "zero"

2. Before archiving & delete the PO. corresponding Material document & invoice document should have deleted?

3. Archiving one - step procedure need to maintain at what scenario?

Regards,

Karan

entry.title: Localization UI text not working in SAP CAP BTP
entry.content:
Hello ,

In my object page I have item table from which if user selects and click on Delete the confirmation pop up should show custom text as "Delete Article {number}?".

I have tried below text change in i18n.properties file but that text is still not changing .
With the same place I was able to change button text and other UI standard text but somehow this is not working 

Below is the code I have tried with 

C_TRANSACTION_HELPER_OBJECT_PAGE_CONFIRM_DELETE_WITH_OBJECTTITLE_SINGULAR|to_MucItems=Delete Article test?
C_TRANSACTION_HELPER_OBJECT_PAGE_CONFIRM_DELETE_WITH_OBJECTTITLE_PLURAL|to_MucItems=Delete Article test ?
# C_TRANSACTION_HELPER_CONFIRM_DELETE_WITH_OBJECTINFO|to_MucItems=Delete Article {0?
# C_TRANSACTION_HELPER_CONFIRM_DELETE_WITH_OBJECTTITLE_ONLY|to_MucItems=Delete Article {0?
# DELETE_SELECTED_ITEM|to_MucItems=Delete Article ?
# DELETE_SELECTED_ITEMS|to_MucItems=Delete Articles?
C_TRANSACTION_HELPER_OBJECT_PAGE_CONFIRM_DELETE_WITH_OBJECTINFO|to_MucItems=Delete Article ?
Where to_MucItems is the composition inside MucHeader entity 

Let me know if something I need to correct this or someone has a working example I can refer to

entry.title: SAP B1 mobile app approvals are not showing in List
entry.content:
Dear Expert,
 
In SAP B1 mobile app approvals are not showing in List. It is showing No data.
 
Following is the error message.
 
com.sap.b1i.xcellerator.XcelleratorException: XCE001 Nested exception: com.sap.b1i.bizprocessor.BizProcException: BPE001 Nested exception: com.sap.b1i.xcellerator.XcelleratorException: XCE001 Nested exception: com.sap.b1i.xcellerator.RejectedException: XCE208 Resource is busy B1Server|company ="Company Name"
 
The same functionality is working fine for another database on the same server.
 
Regards,
Shubham Bulley

entry.title: Re: Bundles Now Live in SAP for Me - A Breakthrough in Outage Communication!
entry.content:
This feature can be very helpful for our internal team responsible for the Service Management and Monitoring, in order to see in a clear picture which application is affected by a specific disruption.
One of the very difficult topics usually is to identify which applications are affected by a disruption of a service.
Many thanks for this! I'll start to play with it 😁
Davide

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 4 - Mobile device capabilities
entry.content: Today is the official end date for Build Apps Challenge and from tomorrow a different topic challenge starts. However, you can continue to complete the challenge beyond today.

entry.title: How to find the budget of the PM orders instead of finding them in IMR1 report ?
entry.content: Can anyone tell me how to check the budget of the pm orders instead of finding them in IMR1 report

entry.title: Re: How to keep users logged in after hybris upgrading ?
entry.content:
Fixed it by overriding spring-security-core-5.8.5.jar  and org.springframework.security:spring-security-web . 
Took old classes from spring-***-***-5.6.1.jars and placed tham in new  spring-***-***-5.8.5.jars by java command that helps to modify jars:
jar uf spring-security-core-5.8.5.jar org/springframework/security/authentication/UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken.class org/springframework/security/core/authority/SimpleGrantedAuthority.class 
Made configuration so that my jars (~/core-customize/hybris/config/customize/platform/ext/core/lib/spring-security-core-5.8.5.jar and spring-security-web-5.8.5.jar ) override platform jars.

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Datasphere Version 2024.9 — Apr 23, 2024
entry.content:
Hello @MKreitlein,

Thanks for the reply and the information. I think it's still a good practice to re-deploy from bottom to top (view to AM). 🙂

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Automatic account determination
entry.content:
Dear community,

Our client have a special requirement for the account determination on material on S4 Public Cloud.

He needs to have the following schemes :

For material entry :

Debit on stock account (BSX)

Credit on supplier account  (WRX)


For stock consumption :

Debit on charge account (EKG)

Credit on stock (BSX)

 

For invoice entry :

Debit on supplier account (WRX)

Credit on final supplier account

 

Usually, EKG is used for material entry, not for stock consumption.

 

They do not use stock variation account.

My question is can we change on Public cloud the standard schemes  ?

Thank you for your help!

 

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Automatic Replenishment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Warehouse Management
entry.content:
Hi,

Does Auto - Repl will work for ST for which HU required is mandatory?

entry.title: Pricing Determination in SAP S/4HANA On-Premise System
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working with SAP S/4HANA On-Premise system and have a question regarding pricing determination.

Pricing Determination: I understand that there is an existing pricing determination routine in SAP S/4HANA. However, I am curious to know if this is the only way to implement pricing in SAP S/4HANA On-Premise system. Is it possible to use other methods for pricing determination? Can this be used as an alternative to the old pricing determination routine? As we are planning to do in Clean core way.

As per traditional way we can implement the Calculation routine using VOFM Tcode and configure the same in Pricing procedure.

Best Regards,
Padmakar Kudtarkar

entry.title: How to restrict employees with admingroup to delete some type from list view.
entry.content:
But allow to delete this type  to commerce core team (commercecore usergroup) . 
I managed to do this for all groups :

<context type="StoreLocatorFeature" component="collectioneditorareaactions" merge-by="type">
    <y:actions xmlns:y="http://www.hybris.com/cockpit/config/hybris">
        <y:group qualifier="common">
            <y:action action-id="com.hybris.cockpitng.action.delete" merge-mode="remove"/>
            <y:action action-id="de.hybris.platform.apiregistrybackoffice.actions.deletedestinationtarget" property="selectedObjects" merge-mode="append"/>
        </y:group>
    </y:actions>
</context>

How to do this ?

What is the current AI usage limit? Is this a limitation of the pilot service as this blog suggests?

entry.title: Re: Print custom page range from Crystal Report Viewer

entry.title: Re: How to show custom Data in a Fiori App (RAP)
entry.content:
Yes, You can create a custom table and custom data in class implementation.

Please refer to this link, It has detailed steps

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-persistence.html

entry.title: Re: Batch number in purchase order
entry.content: If batch management is active, it is logical that a batch number is assigned upon Goods Receipt. If Quality Inspection is mandatory, the Usage Decision will finally determine what happens to that Batch.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hello from Mannheim. I'm Phuong Anh from SAP Walldorf.

entry.title: HU and product task for outbound
entry.content:
Hello colleagues,

I have a question that I'm stuck on at the moment. I need HU warehouse tasks for the delivery. All of a sudden, only product warehouse tasks are created. I know you can set it at the level of storage type or storage process type. But I don't know exactly where at the moment.
Can someone show me the corresponding fields please?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build apps, HTML 5 issue
entry.content:
It sounds like you're encountering an "Internal Server Error" when trying to access your SAP Build App apps deployed to the Business Technology Platform (BTP). Here's how you can troubleshoot this issue:

1. Check BTP Log: Start by checking the logs on the BTP to see if there are any error messages or stack traces that provide clues about what went wrong. Look for any recent errors related to your deployed apps.

2. Review Recent Changes: Even if you think you didn't change anything, some configuration settings might have been altered unintentionally. Review any recent changes you or your team have made to configurations, permissions, or the app itself.

3. Ensure App Deployment: Double-check that your apps are still deployed correctly on the BTP. Sometimes deployments can get disrupted or lost, especially if there were updates or maintenance on the BTP.

4. Check App Dependencies: Ensure that any dependencies your apps rely on, such as databases or external services, are accessible and functioning properly. If there were any changes to these dependencies, it could affect your app's functionality.

5. Review App Code: If you have access to the source code of your apps, review it for any potential issues. Look for syntax errors, incorrect configurations, or any changes that could cause the internal server error.

6. Configuration Check: Make sure all configurations, including environment variables, are correctly set up for your apps in the BTP. Verify that the app is pointing to the right endpoints and resources.

7. Test on Different Devices/Browsers: Sometimes, the error might be specific to certain devices or browsers. Test your apps on different devices and browsers to see if the issue persists across all of them.

8. Check SAP Build Status: Check if there are any reported issues or maintenance activities on SAP Build's status page. It's possible that there might be a known issue affecting the service.

9. Contact Support: If you're unable to resolve the issue after trying the above steps, reach out to SAP support for assistance. Provide them with as much information as possible, including error messages, logs, and any relevant details about your environment and setup.

By following these steps, you should be able to identify and resolve the "Internal Server Error" you're encountering with your SAP Build App apps deployed on the BTP.

Here are the best HTML online learning platforms:
1. W3School  2. Iqra Technology  3. javatPoint

entry.title: More About Cloud-Ready Mode in SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Benefit from cloud-ready mode in SAP Master Data Governance

As mentioned in my recent blog post, cloud-ready mode was introduced with SAP S/4HANA 2023 and is the go-to operation mode for companies that are just getting started with the governance of business partner data with SAP Master Data Governance (MDG). For companies that already use MDG, we recommend evaluating the benefits of switching to cloud-ready mode. Cloud-ready mode is based on ABAP Cloud, and it’s the future-geared development environment for all MDG deployments. 

To find out more about how it relates to classic mode in processing business partner data, you may check out this technical article that provides a deep-dive comparison of both operation modes.

MDG technical article

To open the article, click the picture above.

Find out more about cloud-ready mode:

  • Overview presentation
  • FAQs

Stay on track and simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

Best, 
Markus

entry.title: Bad debt reserve calculation with TCode: F107
entry.content:
When calculating the bad debt using TCode F107 in SAP, the accounts displayed are not mentioned in OBB0 - B03 (Flat-rate individual value adjustment). 

1. Maintained percentages in OB_7

2. Mentioned accounts in OBB0

3. Assigned Value adj.Key for the Customer master record

4. Maintained Field Status Group: G067 for the Reconciliation and Alternative accounts in FS00

No need to calculate bad debt reserve for specific Reconciliation accounts, but F107 calculated and posted the FI document on it.

Question: Does F107 calculate whether all accounts have G067 or not? Is there any step missed?

entry.title: Backoffice User Restrictions not working for Components
entry.content:
Hi,

We have created a backoffice user restriction with user 'itsupport' of employee group.
We want this user to have access to few of the Components, so we tried giving access to all possible component tables. Attaching the impex file in attachments for reference. But when we search for Components in filter tree of backoffice we are getting no read access for all the components. Attaching screenshot for the same.

Thanking you in advance.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Backoffice User Restrictions not working for Components
entry.content:
It might be fine to look at only the readable and writable catalog version data by referring the below guide:
 
Backoffice Visibility Controls
If it is only some components in the catalog version, you may have to add your own restriction.

entry.title: API Management: Unlocking OAuth Strategies
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's rapidly evolving enterprise landscape, the API-first approach has emerged as a cornerstone for organizations striving to stay competitive and relevant. At the heart of this approach lies API management, a central place for the orchestration, optimization, and security of APIs.

In this blog, I will elaborate the authorization considerations surrounding APIs, as well as the OAuth strategies I've investigated and encountered during my learning journey and professional experience.

If you are new to API management I highly recommend going through the help doc to get basic knowledge of API management capability before diving into this blog.

Through out the blog, I have added links to some learning journeys / blogs which you can refer. I thought this is better instead of rewriting the same content which is already well written.

I will structure the blog in below sections

  1. Pre-requisites
  2. Creating a simple integration flow
  3. Securing integration flows
    1. Exploring API Management OAuth Policy
    2. Extending OAuth Beyond API Management
  4. Testing steps to test both solution approaches.
  5. Conclusion

Pre-requisites

  1. SAP BTP account or trail account
  2. Integration Suite Tenant with below capabilities
    1. Cloud Integration
    2. API Management (ensure you have proper roles assigned)
  3. Process Integration Runtime service
  4. Insomnia or Postman client

Creating simple integration flow

I created a simple integration flow and deployed it as a REST API in the cloud integration tenant. This integration flow does following things

1. Logs the incoming message as attachment

2. Persists the message in JMS queue

Note - This is the basic integration flow. Consider it as a mock iflow which can be replaced by any other iflow based on the business use case.

Figure-1: Integration Flow

Securing integration flows 

In an enterprise landscape, you will always come across situations, that you have to expose your integration flows using API management. Ofcourse Integration suite provides API management capability which can seamlessly wrap the integration flows and create an API proxy.

One of the important question always comes up - How to secure your APIs/iflows? The obvious answer is OAuth 2.0 (Open Authorization 2.0).

Here I will showcase, 2 solution approaches to secure the API with OAuth 2.0. 

  1. Exploring API Management OAuth Policy: API Management provides security policies which can be used out of the box.
    1. Create an API Proxy. Configure -- APIs -- OAuthService. This Service will be used to generate the token. Follow Step 1 (1 - Create the Token Endpoint) from this blog
    2. Create another API - LogMessageAPI as a proxy to the iflow. We will add token validation in this proxy. Define the API by referring the Step 2: Define the API from this learning journey.
    3. In the LogMessageAPI:
      1. Add policy template to the LogMessageAPI as mentioned in Step 3, 4 and 5 of this learning journey
      2. Add VerifyAccessToken policy as the first policy in the pre-flow of the ProxyEndpoint using OAuthV2 in the LogMessageAPI proxy. 
        <OAuthV2 async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
   <!-- this flag has to be set when you want to work with third-party access tokens -->
   <ExternalAuthorization>false</ExternalAuthorization>
   <!-- valid values are GenerateAccessToken, GenerateAccessTokenImplicitGrant, GenerateAuthorizationCode ,
    RefreshAccessToken , VerifyAccessToken , InvalidateToken , ValidateToken  -->
   <Operation>VerifyAccessToken</Operation>
   <GenerateResponse enabled="true"/><SupportedGrantTypes/>
   <Tokens/>
</OAuthV2>
    4. Once you deploy the OAuthService and LogMessageAPI and publish the product to API Business Hub Enterprise, you will get the application key and application secret which can be used as client id and client secret to get the token.
    5. In this way, you can use the inbuilt OAuth policy to generate the OAuth token and to validate the token for other API proxies.
  2. Extending OAuth Beyond API Management: Let us dive deeper into another approach where we can use the process integration runtime OAuth server to generate the token through OAuthService API. 
    1. Create an API Proxy. Configure -- APIs -- OAuthService.
    2. Import the policy template in the pre-flow of the ProxyEndpoint. I have exported my policy template and is available in GitHub - https://github.com/avinashvaidya09/apimanagementpolicytemplate 
    3. Each policy is listed and briefly described below - 
      1. lookupOAuthToken - Using LookupCache policy, checks if access token is available in cache.
      2. extractRequestHeader - Using ExtractVariables policy, extracts the client id and client secret from the request headers.
      3. getBTPOAuthMetadata - Using KeyValueMapOperations policy, finds the BTP oauth token url saved in the key value map
      4. setAuthorization - Using BasicAuthentication policy, creates Authorization object.
      5. callBTPOAuthService - Using ServiceCallout policy, calls external BTP OAuth token url.
      6. raiseException - Using raiseException policy, raises exception if BTP OAuth token url returns exception.
      7. extractVariablesFromOAuthResponse - Using ExtractVariables policy, extracts token response.
      8. populateOAuthToken - Using PopulateCache policy, populates access token in cache which will be used by point 1.
    4. Go to your BTP subaccount -- Create instance of Process Integration Runtime.
    5. Create a service key and get the OAuth metadata
      1. client id
      2. client secret
      3. token url
    6. Add the token url in the key value map in API configuration to make it externalized. This token url will be used in the service call out policy - callBTPOAuthService
    7. Save and and deploy the OAuthService.
    8. Ensure the integration flow is deployed. Get the endpoint URL.
    9. Create another API - LogMessageAPI as a proxy to the iflow. Define the API by referring the Step 2: Define the API from this learning journey.
    10. In this API, you do not have to define any access token validation policy, but the bearer token will be forwarded to the integration flow and will be validated directly by process integration runtime.

Test your APIs

  • Download any REST client like - Insomnia or Postman.
  • Deploy the API proxies and get the endpoints
  1. Testing approach - 1:
    1. Go to API Business Hub Enterprise. Create Application and add the product containing both the API proxies.
    2. Get the application secret and application key after the application is created.
    3. Create new HTTP request for API proxy OAuthService endpoint.
    4. Add client id - client id will be the application key from point 2.
    5. Add client secret - client secret will be the application secret from point 2. 
    6. Add grant type - client_credentials
    7. Add Content-Type - application/x-www-form-urlencoded
    8. Get the access token.
    9. Create new HTTP request with API management LogMessageAPI endpoint. 
      1. Add access token
      2. Add sample JSON payload
    10. Execute the request.
    11. Go to integration suite - Monitor - Integration & APIs. You should see a message in the queue. Click on the message id and you will land on the executed iflow with payload attached for reference.
  2. Testing approach - 2:
    1. Create new HTTP request with API management OAuthService endpoint.
    2. Add client id - client id from process integration runtime service key.
    3. Add client secret - client secret from process integration runtime service key.
    4. Content-Type - application/x-www-form-urlencoded
    5. Get the access token
    6. Create new HTTP request with API management LogMessageAPI endpoint. 
      1. Add access token
      2. Add sample JSON payload
    7. Execute the request.
    8. Go to integration suite - Monitor - Integration & APIs - Message Queues. You should see a messages in the queue. Click on the message id and you will land on the executed iflow with payload attached for reference.

Conclusion

Drawing from my experience, I must emphasize that these insights can serve as a starting point, though their applicability may vary based on specific scenarios.

This is just a glimpse of wide set of capabilities offered by SAP Integration Suite and API management. Stay curious! Keep learning!

To get more updates about this topic, please follow the below pages

  • SAP Integration Suite
  • API Management
  • Blogs

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me – avinash.vaidya@sap.com

entry.title: Re: How to show custom Data in a Fiori App (RAP)
entry.content: Thats not exactly what i meant. In the Tutorial you linked, the Fiori List App just shows Data which is in a Database. Yes you can write a abap class and add Data to that Data Table manualy, but i want to change the Data in the Fiori App, when someone searches for something in the Fiori List App.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative May 2024 Bootcamp Guide

Please how could I solve this?

Many thanks. Stephane

entry.title: SAP SALES versus SAP CRM versus S4HANA Sales Versus SAP SD
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am very confused at various products SAP provides.

Can anybody guide with difference between SAP SALES versus SAP CRM versus S4HANA Sales Versus SAP SD at very broad level and which comes under cloud.

Does SAP SALES come for free with S4HANA cloud or it is S4HANA sales which is similar to SD comes with S4HANA?

Does SAP CRM Cloud come under SAP SALES?

What is C4C then?

SAP SALES has Configure, Quotation which is similar to S4HANA SD, then why in different products?

Is SAP CRM Cloud same as Business suit CRM?

Kindly help with removal of confusion cloud.

Regards.

entry.title: Labs Talk: Customer Experience & Web 3
entry.content:
The upcoming episode of Labs Talk podcast, scheduled for May 15th at 16:00 CET, promises an engaging exploration into the intersection of Customer Engagement and Web 3. Hosted by Sven Haiges and Andreas Neumeier, the discussion will feature Scott Young, Head of Web 3 at SAP, as a distinguished guest.

In this episode, the panel will delve into the dynamic landscape of Web3 technologies, shedding light on emerging trends, such as NFTs, and their implications for businesses. Scott Young, with his expertise, will offer valuable insights into the integration of Web3 with SAP Customer Experience solutions, including Commerce, Sales & Service, and Marketing (emarsys).

The conversation is set to unravel the potential synergies between Web3 and traditional customer engagement strategies, exploring avenues for leveraging digital twins and other innovative approaches. By addressing key topics like Web3 utility and its integration with SAP's robust ecosystem, the discussion aims to equip listeners with a comprehensive understanding of the evolving digital landscape.

Join us for this enriching dialogue, where industry leaders converge to explore the future of customer engagement in the era of Web3. Stay tuned for updates on how to access the live stream of this insightful discussion.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hello @gsauter welcome and great you found your way to SAP Community. You might also want to join the Paris Group to connect with other locals in Paris. There are also oftentimes events organized, so it's a great opportunity to meet other community members in person!

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hello @AnhK and welcome! I am also working for SAP in Walldorf, but I live close to Mannheim. Nice to meet you. You might want to subscribe to the SAP Community Event calendar  - there are sometimes events in this region, like in June, there will be 3 events in a week: reCAP, UI5con and ABAP Conf. Check these out! It's a great opportunity to learn and to connect with other community members.

very interesting blog.

Thanks and best regards
Axel

entry.title: Re: Updating contract Payment terms
entry.content:
It seems you are using a SD contract BAPI (VA42) to update a MM purchase contract (ME32K) ?

  • What is your exact requirement
  • For MM contract use BAPI_CONTRACT_CHANGE
  • Move the LOOP at RETURN table for error message (type 'E') BEFORE the COMMIT.

entry.title: How to access Hanan Spatial Service (HSS) APIs from one Subaccount in BAS of another Subaccount?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have created Hana Spatial Services (HSS) along with Service Key in one subaccount and trying to access it in the BAS of another Subaccount on same BTP.

We are trying to access the APIs like described here: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-spatial-services-apis.html

But we are getting the below error:

Connection is being rejected. The service isn’t running on the server, or incorrect proxy settings in vscode, or a firewall is blocking requests. Details: RequestError: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:443.

Could you please suggest on what extra config we might need in order to access the HSS on one subaccount from HTTP Client of BAS on another subaccount.  

Thanks,

Bhavyasri M

entry.title: Timesheet 3.0 forced to enter activity Type
entry.content:
Hi SAP Guru,

We are at the moment upgrading SAP from version 1809 to 2023 with this we are also upgrading SAP Fiori Timesheet 2.0 to SAP Fiori Timesheet 3.0.

in the old version Timesheet 2.0 we where allowed to enter our time with only an Att./Absence type and controlling area.

in the new version of the timesheet we need to enter Att./Absence type, Controlling area, Activity Type and reciever costcenter or else it wont post the time entry

Does anyone know how to set these settings correctly so that we can book the hours as before? Or does anyone know what caused this to change?

the settings of the cat profile are as follows:

 

i hope you can help me

Davey

entry.title: Re: SAP Service Level Agreement Credit Claim made easy in SAP for Me

Hello:

This blog is helpful. Question for you. I've seen 30 business days for the credit window. That's what's reflected on this KBA as well: https://me.sap.com/notes/2829755/E.

Thoughts?

Thanks.

entry.title: API for project WEB
entry.content:
Hi, I'm an intern at a Brazilian company, and I have been doing a web project, which I would need to integrate with SAP ERP, to get information about some transactions that are in the ERP, and I would also probably do an integration to set some values ​​directly,
Is it possible to get this API?
I started doing the frontend, but I know I need to see what information I can get

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere + SAP S/4HANA: Your Guide to Seamless Data Integration
entry.content:
Hi @jeetendrakapase,

Thanks for a great blog post. CDS Views as a first choice should be a principal - crucial point.

I have a question regarding CDS Views: As you mentioned ABAP CDS Views provides business data along with semantic (context) information (hierarchies, text, etc.), security, associations, and improved performance -- Do we get semantic information in Datasphere when we use CDS views as source for remote tables, replication flows, data flows? OR Do we get semantic information when we use only Model Import method for CDS Views as source?

As you mentioned that Bypassing CDS views to replicate tables into SAP Datasphere directly is not recommended. The preferred approach is to leverage the existing, comprehensive CDS data models whenever possible.  In which scenario might replicating tables be unavoidable due to specific business or compliance requirements? I can only think of when the source system is SAP ECC. Can't we just create custom CDS views for the tables and still use CDS views if our system is SAP S/4HANA? So we can abandon using tables as source and get relief from maintaining SAP LT Replication Server and its components?

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Getting Started with Spaces and Pages
entry.content:
Authors: Julia Queck, Kattia Jordan-Philipp

As a business user you can now benefit from a seamless and enjoyable user experience. If you are just getting started with spaces and pages, and My Home then the following demo videos are for you.
Watch how to access relevant applications in an easy and structured way with Spaces and Pages, and see how to use My Home to combine information, tasks, and your favorite applications so you can access exactly what you need to get your job done with speed and agility.

 

Welcome to Spaces and Pages

In this video, we introduce spaces and pages and show you how to navigate between them.

 

Personalizing Pages

This video shows you how easy it is to personalize your pages, for example by adding, hiding, or rearranging applications. It also shows you how to pin applications from pages to My Home.

Changing the Sequence of Spaces

In this video, we show you how easy it is to pin, unpin and rearrange your spaces in the menu to accommodate your individual needs and preferences.

 

Toggle between Spaces and Pages and Groups

This video is relevant for you if you are transitioning from the former groups to spaces and pages - and if your organization allows you still to toggle between groups, and spaces and pages. Watch the video to see how to use the toggling option.

 

My Home

In this video, we show you how easy it is to use My Home to manage your work, and to personalize your favorites pages, apps and insights to fit your needs.

Further blog posts:

Getting Familiar with Spaces and Pages in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Elevate Your SAP Cloud ALM Experience: Introducing the New ‘’Launchpad’’ and ‘’Welcome App’’
entry.content:
Are you tired of feeling like your platform needs to be more cohesive and connected? Do you need help finding the necessary tools and resources to streamline your workflow and maximize productivity? Yearning for a simpler, more intuitive experience? Look no further! 

 We're excited to announce the launch of the revamped Launchpad for SAP Cloud ALM, and the introduction of the new Welcome app. This revamped Launchpad offers simplified navigation and a fresh design. And the Welcome app? It's your go-to resource for mastering the app and its components. Join us as we explore the exciting features and enhancements poised to elevate your user experience to new heights. 

Revamped Launchpad: Your Gateway to Enhanced User Experience 

In the ever-evolving landscape of digital platforms, user experience reigns supreme. That's why we've given our Launchpad a fresh new look and feel, all based on extensive user feedback.  

 Key Features 

Tab-based spaces: SAP Cloud ALM now organizes each area – Implementation, Operations, Service, Transformation and Administration into its own dedicated space, offering a tailored experience that caters to your specific requirements. Within each space, groups are available offering a streamlined approach to locating applications within the same category. 

Centralized location: "My Home" serves as a centralized location, offering insights into SAP Cloud ALM and providing quick links to valuable resources, enabling you to jump start your experience. Customize this space by adding your frequently used applications tiles to the launchpad and arranging them as desired, transforming it into your go-to central entry point. 

Onboarding experience: Kickstart your journey with the "Get to know SAP Cloud ALM" app, stationed on the first page as the first tile, providing a comprehensive overview of the platform and its capabilities, ensuring you hit the ground running. 

Enhanced support: Stay connected with quick access to help pages through small "Link Tiles" placed for your convenience. 

 

 

Welcome to the SAP Cloud ALM Welcome App

Congratulations on taking the first step towards unleashing the full potential of SAP Cloud ALM with the Welcome app. This dedicated personal guide is designed to help you navigate through every step of the onboarding process. From configuring essential settings to understanding key concepts like Implementations, Operations, and Services, the Welcome app has got you covered. 

 Key Features 

Adoption use cases: We believe in learning by example. That's why we've curated a collection of adoption use cases to guide users through best practices. With slides and video support, you'll gain valuable insights into real-world scenarios, empowering you to make informed decisions and maximize the potential of SAP Cloud ALM. 

Functional overview: Understanding the ins and outs of SAP Cloud ALM is essential for leveraging its full capabilities. Our functional overview provides a comprehensive breakdown of the platform's different areas, giving you a clear roadmap to navigate its features confidently.  

Further links: Directs you to a wealth of help material, ensuring you have access to the information you need at your fingertips. 

Seamless Transition: We guide users from SAP for Me provisioning to the Welcome App within SAP Cloud ALM, ensuring a seamless transition and easy access to the app. 

 

 

Future Updates 

We're excited to continuously improve the Welcome App based on user feedback and usability tests. Our goal is to turn this app into a one-stop solution for setting up and using SAP Cloud ALM, making it as simple and efficient as possible for you. 

 Join Us on the Journey 

Your feedback is invaluable in shaping the Welcome app into an even more valuable resource for you and the entire user community. We believe in the power of collaboration, and the Welcome App stands as a testament to that belief. Together, let's embark on a journey that demystifies the complexities of SAP Cloud ALM, equipping you with essential tools to thrive. 

Welcome aboard to a world of endless possibilities with SAP Cloud ALM!

entry.title: Integrating RAR and RA (Cost Object Controlling)
entry.content:

I had found this blog from 2017 Cost Object Controlling Integration - SAP RAR1.2 - SAP Community but it is limited to listing how to steps rather than what the end to end process/accounting will be.

Thanks,

Alex

entry.title: Re: Display exchange rates from Request Current Market Da...
entry.content:
Dear User,

You can use the App Currency Exchange Rates (App ID F3616) to view the exchange rates for currency pair.

Fiori Apps Library link

Help Portal link

 

Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2019.001 v1.62 novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content:
Bom dia Pessoal,

Apos aplicar as notas da NT2019 alguns objetos ficaram com erro de ativação entre ele a FM J_1BNFE_IN_MERGE_XML_DATA_400.

REFERENCE(IO_XML_NFE_IN) TYPE REF TO CL_J_1BNFE_XML.

Essa classe CL_J_1BNFE_XML nao contem o atributo IT_NFE_PROD_PRESUMIDO.

Alguém passou por isso?

Grato

entry.title: Multiple entries in a Single table via OData service
entry.content:
Hi.

I tried to create a service in which we have to send more than one entry via OData service.

My project name is ZVBAP and I use VBAP table fields which are vbeln, posnr and matnr. 

So by use of Create entity I can send the single entry to the Standard table but after many try I didn't get any solution to how to send more than one entry in Standard table.

Also I tried this in METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~create_entity but still it doesn't run and it affects the Create entity service also.

 

Please help me to solve this.

Thanks in advance.

Rohan Chauhan

entry.title: What is the use of %cid in RAP entity?
entry.content:
Hi all, I'm developing API based on RAP bo.

In batch request, there could be multi entities as import parameter in one changeset, and in RAP internal table, I see a field %cid. I thought it is content id which was provided in batch request body, but after I tried to set it, it was not shown in debugging.

I think this is the field to differentiate the entity with same key in batch. Please correct me if I was wrong.

Regards,
Eric

entry.title: Re: visual code studio command run failed with error : sp...
entry.content: It worked. Thanks!

entry.content: Hi , are those mobile responsive also? if yes then i am full ready to learn this abap2ui5 completely and create application using abap2ui5 itself 😅

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Overview - Gen AI and Sustainability
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Overview of Webinar Series

In this webinar we have Ulrike Raidl, kicking off the multi-day webinar series. In it, we discuss an array of updates for the 2023 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (FPS0). We delve into the SAP product strategy, the delivery models for Cloud ERP, RISE packaging, the evolution of SAP S/4HANA alongside highlighting significant changes in various lines of business like procurement, asset management and finance. Additionally, new features are coming our way such as integration with Microsoft Teams, an inbuilt collaboration tool, and strategies for enhanced sustainability. 

We also have a glimpse into the future of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Developments to look forward to includes smarter methods for goods receipt, inventive work orders, enhancements in shipping and receiving, along with tools to monitor our carbon footprint. In broader terms, by 2025 we have a planned shift towards central business configuration for SAP S/4HANA installation. Finally, we would like to emphasize that we value your input and are open to suggestions you can provide to SAP using the influence programs. 

SAP portfolio strategy 

Looking on SAP's priority, the Cloud ERP portfolio is based on our business technology platform (SAP BTP) and incorporates innovation and sustainability. SAP offers industry-specific solutions, along with essential business portfolios like HR, spend management, and CRM. The two primary Cloud ERP products are 'GROW with SAP', a standardized public offering for organizations that are on a growth trajectory and happy to work with a highly standardized offering, and 'RISE with SAP', a customizable and adaptable solution tailored to fit unique business needs that helps organizations continue to run on their standards. 

RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition today 

'RISE with SAP' is geared to boost your digital transformation and cloud migration. This private edition offering is tailored to various needs and comes with a single central contract and one Service Level Agreement (SLA). Then, we'll get a sneak peek at the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and a toolkit for digital transformation, loaded with the necessary infrastructure, security, resources, and migration and adoption services. We'll look at the evolution of SAP S/4HANA since it beginnings in 2015. Lastly, we'll find out about the extended seven-year release strategy and constant innovation promised with SAP S/4HANA. 

2023 Highlights Overview 

We explore the diverse features of 'RISE with SAP' and their 'Premium Plus Edition', which offers additional capabilities for generative AI, sustainability, advanced suite for The Office of the Chief Financial Officer (OCFO) and access to supplier networks. For example, in the area of procurement, we'll delve into how the recent improvements can shape our sustainability efforts and keep track of our carbon footprint. Or, in the area of manufacturing, we'll get to discover the new advanced scheduling board for production orders. Or, in the Cross Topics area, we'll look at how Microsoft Teams integrated with an in-house Collaboration Manager feature boost our teamwork and communication. 

Outlook on upcoming innovations 

We'll get a sneak peek into what's in the pipeline for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, including boosting usability, intelligent scenarios leveraging our digital assistant Joule and significant investment in reporting on green data with our carbon accounting innovation . Plus, customers can check out the product roadmap that shows what's next for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, what new things are coming up, and what their big ideas are for 2025. From the Co-Innovation Program, Continuous Influence Program, to Customer Engagement Initiatives, Beta Testing, Integration Experience Feedback, and User Groups, Early Adopter Care, can genuinely shape innovation's path forward. 

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

 

SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Sales
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this webinar, we have Christine Hofmann, Solution Expert of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, offering a well-rounded review of the 2023 release of S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in Sales. Let's have a look at how sales is evolving, interesting things about solution quotations and SAP's billing and revenue management, and how a customer can have a say in SAP's development plans. 

We first explore updates in sales, focusing on role-based access for pricing elements, improved product returns, Fiori apps updates, and the use of spreadsheets for contract uploads. We also discuss changes in available-to-promise (ATP) results and upcoming platform extensions. Then, we look at solution quotations with a new multi-level configuration. In the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management section, we discuss advancements in Subscription Order Management, Contract Accounting, and Convergent Invoicing. Finally, we glimpse SAP's roadmap and see how we can influence the future of the products. 

Key features and enhancements 

  1. Role-Based Access Controls: Introducing accurate access controls to price and cost details in sales and billing documents. This feature safeguards the business from information misuse, delivering relevant data access to authorized staff, and preventing unnecessary access. 

  2. Flexible Customer Return Procedures: With the 2023 release, we can use a return product while the valuation process is pending, enhancing the user experience and ensuring a smooth and easy return process. 

  3. Sales Contract Creation via Spreadsheet: Simplifying sales contract management, the 2023 release enables creation of sales contracts through spreadsheet uploads, saving time and manual entry efforts. 

  4. Advanced Configuration in Solution Quotations: The update brings a new feature that lets us set up solution quotations for subscription products with multi-level advanced variant configuration. Many innovations also let us see info about price groups, subscription billing, plan cycles, and Work Breakdown Structure elements (WBS elements) right in our solution quotations. 

  5. Refined ATP Analysis in Sales Order: With this 2023 release, we enable the analysis and adoption of confirmed delivery dates and quantities on the Availability-To-Promise (ATP) results screen for sales-order items in the SAP Fiori app. 

  6. Expansion of BRIM: There are new features in subscription order management like adding project breakdown elements, setting payment deadlines, adjusting refunds, and support for multiple configurations. Upgrades in contract accounting and convergent invoicing include longer contract periods, linking payment functions, a more adaptable approval flow, and more flexibility for billing plans.
     
  7. Future Roadmap: The SAP Roadmap provides a comprehensive view of the planned enhancements and features for future releases, painting a vivid picture of where SAP aims to take its products. 

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Finance
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary  

In this webinar, we have Michel Haesendonckx discussing the strategic product direction, and planned and delivered innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 in the Finance line of business.

Key features, enhancements and roadmap items 

  1. Financial Planning & Analysis: This process supports strategic planning by allowing ad-hoc analysis and providing insights into costs and margins to understand financial performance. This increases confidence in business plans with the help of reliable data. 
  2. Margin Reporting: This feature allows us to track down details not only on Profit & Loss but also on specific balance sheet items. 
  3. 3. Dashboards: There are enhancements in the delivery of complex dashboards that combine information from different sources. These dashboards are designed based on finance foundation in SAP Datasphere directly integrated to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, to provide more robust financial content from different areas.  
  4. 4. SAP S/4HANA Finance Foundation: This feature provides an end-to-end finance foundation within the SAP Datasphere application, enabling the combination of information from different sources, even those residing outside the S/4HANA system. 
  5. 5. Flexibility in Defining Market Segments: The new release offers more flexibility in defining and updating market segments, independent of the operating concern. 
  6. 6. Profit Center Valuations: The additional ledger for profit center valuation, pricing agreements between profit centers, and the elimination of intercompany margins, allows profit centers to trade at arm's length using transfer pricing. 
  7. 7. Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA): The UPA is a key innovation that extends the ledger concept not only to financial accounting but also to controlling. With UPA, we can establish a parallel value flow across end-to-end processes. 
  8. 8. Carbon Accounting: This is a pioneering innovation that allows transactional carbon accounting within the S/4HANA system as part of the Universal Journal. This is part of the Green Ledger approach where sustainability information is treated similarly to financial data. 
  9. 9. Record to Report: This process ensures that every financial accounting entry is event-based, resulting in reduced manual intervention and improved financial reporting. 
  10. 10. Financial Closing: Enhancements have been made in defining validation rules and the automation of certain tasks in the financial closing process. 
  11. 11. Enterprise Service Management: This is a next-generation solution supporting end-to-end shared services automation with Generative AI, making it particularly useful for Shared Service Organizations. 
  12. 12. International Trade and Global Tax Management: The release also includes enhancements in trade compliance, tax compliance, and documentary reporting compliance. 
  13. 13. Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC): There's a focus on integrating risk management and financial information, facilitated by SAP Financial Compliance Management solution. 

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Research and Development
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording. 

Summary

In this webinar, we have Vinay V, discussing the latest release highlights of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 in the Research and Development (R&D) line of business. It is a broad area that includes project control (including project logistics and project financial control – classically the area that Project Systems covered), and product engineering as the core areas. R&D also includes product compliance, product lifecycle management (PLM), portfolio and project management, and circular economy. The 2023 release brings improvements in many of these areas. 

Key features and enhancements 

  1. Project Control: With the 2023 release project cash management is made to acces the Universal Journal directly. Also, new integration of responsibility management into project systems enables efficient team assignment for workflow and situation handling, etc. 

  2. Product Development Foundation: A new item category called a 'non-plant item' has been introduced to add services and project items without needing a plant assignment. It simplifies the set-up, especially in service-based organizations. 

  3. Change Management: Taking feedback from customers, we have provided the ability to add the same component multiple times with different relevancy into a change record. This is useful especially for long-running change processes, providing transparency over different phases of product change. 

  4. Integration with External PLM Systems: Enhanced integration with external Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) systems is made possible through seamless bi-directional document exchange between SAP solutions to an external PLM system. 

  5. Product Compliance: In the context of substance management, better management of customer-specific phrases (“called company-owned phrases") is now available. This reduces complexity, eliminates duplicates, and ensures synchronization for effective compliance management. 

  6. Listed substances provisioning through regulatory content service: This update ensures organizations to stay informed with the latest listed substances and listed substance group assignments. 

  7. Substance Volume Tracking: Enriched control and flexibility are offered for substance volume tracking processes. This functionality helps organizations comply with regulatory requirements by recording quantities of sensitive substances. 

  8. Safety Data Sheets Handling: Enhanced handling of multiple and international addresses in safety data sheets results in efficient and regulation-compliant documentation and labelling – for example, printing of product names in English and Chinese. 

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Supply Chain
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary 

In this webinar, we have Vinay V, delving into highlights of new features and enhancements included in the 2023 release of SAP S/4HANA in the supply chain line of business. From inventory management to order promising, we have covered a wide range of scope in this release. The logistics area sees advancements like composite barcode scanning support and the introduction of advanced shipping and receiving processes. For sustainability, we also introduced capabilities for emissions tracking in transportation management. The 2023 release also includes the introduction of a new business process scheduling framework and further improvements to the packaging framework. 

Key features and enhancements 

  1. Composite Barcode Scanning: We have enhanced the ability to scan barcodes to recognize composite barcodes, which contain more information than their one-dimensional ones. This includes material information, global trade information, batch information, serial information, and more. 

  2. Advanced Shipping and Receiving: We have streamlined end-to-end processes for transporting and warehousing products. The integration of embedded warehouse management and transportation management within a single system has simplified many logistics processes. There's also updated integration to Defects and Notifications in Decentralized SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM). 

  3. Unified Package Builder: The unified package builder for handling packaging material has been further enhanced, making it consumable in the context of core logistics for both outbound and inbound deliveries. 

  4. Warehouse Management with Robotics: The integration of warehouse management with a native cloud-based solution called Warehouse Robotics is a significant development in warehouse automation. In larger warehouses, this can be extended to fleet management systems. 

  5. Sustainability in Transportation Management: There’s a new ability to track greenhouse gas emissions in the area of transportation. This helps support sustainability initiatives while providing clear indications of emission performance. 

  6. Order Promising with Availability Change Log (ACL): The availability change log allows changes in supply and demand to be factored into order promising. This provides better order promising capabilities for customers. 

  7. Business Process Scheduling: A new logistics-based scheduling framework, considering different scheduling algorithms used in transportation management, is introduced. This can provide better predictability of material availability. 

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

entry.title: Re: Enchance SAP BI platform CMS system database.unx
entry.content:
There is not any official documentation that I am aware of.

By trial and error, I have found that it is fairly easy to add top level objects as seen when using Query Builder.

With that being said, you will not be able to pull ODBC information from the CMS system database universe.  The ODBC information is local on a computer or server, not stored in the CMS database.

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Sourcing and Procurement
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this session, we have Michael Deller, taking a deep dive with us into what's new with the 2023 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in the sourcing and procurement line of business. We go through an overview of the functional areas of Sourcing and Procurement, then we zoom in on the latest innovations, all tailored to boost productivity of your operations - from central procurement to product sourcing, and beyond. The innovations include the capability for mass creation or modification of model product specifications, improved accuracy in supplier evaluations through a plant-level factory calendar, and simplification of product sourcing via a dedicated overview page. Additionally, the update also focuses on sustainability with the ability for suppliers to submit carbon footprints. The integration of AI for material group recommendations and provisions for mass changes to centrally managed purchase orders using the SAP Fiori app also adds to the efficiency. Usability enhancements in self-service requisitioning streamline various aspects. We will also refer to SAP Road Map Explorer that helps to get informed of the future of our product road map.

Key features and enhancements

  1. Mass Creation of Model Product Specifications: This new app allows users to import model product specifications using spreadsheets, easily create or modify model product specifications, including up to five levels of item hierarchies that can include products, services, and free text.

  2. Supplier Evaluations: We have added the ability to maintain a factory calendar on the plant level, allowing users to define working and nonworking days more accurately. With this update, nonworking days will no longer be considered for supplier evaluation. Furthermore, you will be able to configure the factory calendar for the calculation of supplier evaluation scores. This enhancement increases the accuracy of calculating supplier evaluation scores for time variance.

  3. Product Sourcing Overview Page: The new features allow sourcing managers to collaborate with stakeholders throughout the sourcing process. They can now easily track and review various sourcing projects and associated quotations with the new overview app.

  4. Collecting and Managing Carbon Footprint Data from Suppliers During Direct Material Sourcing Process: Sourcing managers create  cost breakdown templates, mark relevant materials, let suppliers provide cost breakdown information during bids, and compare this information to determine the best supplier bid. Beyond quantities and qualities, collect carbon related data from your suppliers in the same well-structured way.

  5. AI Material Group Recommendations: Get proposals of recommended material groups with confidence scores based on a machine learning algorithm that analyzes previous purchase orders.

  6. Mass Changes of Procurement: Mass changes of centrally managed purchase requisitions can now be done by using SAP Fiori app in online mode rather than just via Excel uploads.

  7. Usability Enhancements in Self-Service Requisitioning: Numerous smaller updates have been made to improve self-service requisitioning such as Improved Status Tracking, Storage Location, and Ability to Copy Accounting Details.

  8. Road Map Explorer: This is a centrally accessible source for all future development plans for SAP products. The road map explorer allows you to filter depending on product, line of business, which industry or any other criterion relevant to you.

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Manufacturing
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this webinar on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Vinay V and Stefan Gerber explore highlights of new features with release 2023 in the Manufacturing line of business. The discussion covers a range of areas, all meant to streamline our operations from planning to the shop floor. Exciting enhancements include Order Notes Management, Interactive Scheduling Board, and improved Capacity Planning. Meanwhile, managing inputs from multiple storage tanks becomes easier with Tank Planning, and the Time-Dependent Safety Stock and Reorder Point is there to handle seasonality and special situations for report-point replenishment of stocked materials. There are improvements to Advanced Scheduling Board  for smoother planning and rescheduling.

Key Features and Enhancements

  1. Order Notes Management: Improves hand-over from production planning to the shop floor by structuring the notes based on planned orders or in-house production orders.

  2. Interactive Scheduling Board: Enables resource assignment through simple drag and drop, considering all dependencies and constraints.

  3. Capacity Planning Enhancements: Provides updated functionalities including the grouping of work centers, displaying the sales order for made-to-order scenarios, and improvement to the Capacity Scheduling Table app to display the availability date of components.

  4. Tank Planning for Process Manufacturing: Can manage inputs from multiple storage tanks along with a flexible definition of storage tank quantities and their sequencing and the intuitive Fiori UI.

  5. Time-Dependent Safety Stock and Reorder Point: Provides flexibility to define and handle special situations, as well as phases for temporary adjustment of inventory levels like campaigns or seasonal trends, without the need for continuous updates of the master data.

  6. Advanced Scheduling Board: Adds a new interactive Gantt chart that allows for a flexible evaluation of the planning horizon, with the added ability to reschedule and realign interactively. Furthermore, the scope of responsibility now includes work center groups.

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Asset Management
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this webinar, we have Noboru Ota, exploring the latest additions and improvements in the Asset Management line of business with the new 2023 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The priorities for the 2023 release are to assist businesses in achieving a closed loop between asset strategy and execution and to  further enhance the core maintenance management capabilities.

For user experience, we will see system demonstrations on the new features added to mass editing, work packs printing, object lists modifications, and equipment structure configurations. Furthermore, we will look at significant improvements to the work permit process, with new Fiori apps simplifying user interactions to enhance safety measures and precautions throughout the maintenance procedure.

Key Features and Improvements

Key features and enhancements include:

  1.  Mass Editing: We have added mass editing capabilities to an assortment of Fiori apps so that we can edit numerous entries simultaneously, providing a faster and more streamlined way to manage data.

  2. Printing and Output of Work Packs: We can now print multiple shop papers and job cards in one go. You can flexibly group them by various criteria such as work center, maintenance technician, or the technical object being maintained or repaired, and include attachments as part of the printout. This includes auto-attachment based on output and document types.

  3. Adding Object List Entries to Maintenance Items: With the ability to add, remove, or change object list entries within maintenance items, we can have better control and efficiency in managing data.

  4. Configuration of Equipment Structure: An easy way to configure the equipment structure. This feature includes the ability to install new components or dismantle existing ones and record the action details.

  5. Work Permit (Work Clearance Management): With the new Fiori apps, the work permit process is simplified, while still ensuring a safe work environment. This process can be customized as per the organization's needs and uses predefined templates for recurring safety measures. We can link a permit to maintenance orders and use the work permit for search and display it in the resultant list of maintenance orders.

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Service
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this webinar, we have Noboru Ota, exploring how the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 release enhances the service line of business. We will learn about highlights of improvements in service management capabilities in the new release in system demonstrations. The highlights include the new Item-Based Accounting, which provides real-time visibility into profitability and revenues for your service business. New features also cover complex asset maintenance processes with the Service with Advanced Execution and scenarios like inter-company service execution.

Key Features and Improvements

  1. Improved Visibility into Profitability and Revenues Recognition: This new financial integration feature (Item-Based Accounting) offers real-time cost and revenue recording and analysis for service orders and contracts, enhancing reporting and improving margin analysis. It also eliminates the need for period ending activities and data warehouse tools, providing real-time profitability for your service business.

  2. Service with Advanced Execution: This feature offers a full-service process to be divided into a commercial service order and an execution maintenance order, facilitating detailed planning and quote generation based on the simulated actuals. Introducing Configurable Task Lists reduce work planning time by describing a sequence of repeat service activities. Furthermore, we now have a system that supports Resource-Related Billing using a Dynamic Item Processor profile (DIP), transforming actual service costs into billable items.

  3. Inter-company Service Execution: This feature facilitates the modelling of service business procedures, allows different companies to complete various aspects of the service execution process, and automates internal cost transfers.

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service
